title: crepidomanes rupicola
intro: Crepidomanes rupicola, also known as the rock cap or rupicola, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of the world. It is often found growing on tree trunks, branches, and rocks. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. Crepidomanes rupicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists for its potential health benefits.
